strategic_profile.executive_summary: Lehigh University is a highly selective, medium-sized private research university (R1) located in Bethlehem, Pennsylvania. Known for its rigorous engineering and business programs, Lehigh fosters a collaborative yet intensive academic environment where interdisciplinary studies, dual degrees, and undergraduate research are heavily emphasized.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The overall acceptance rate has dropped from nearly 50% in 2020 to roughly 26% for the Class of 2028, largely driven by a record number of applications (>20,000).
analyst_takeaways.implication: Lehigh is returning to historical levels of selectivity, making it a highly competitive target/reach school rather than a safety.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Graduates boast a median 10-year earnings of $101,748 according to the College Scorecard, paired with relatively modest median debt.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Lehigh offers exceptional Return on Investment (ROI), particularly for students in the College of Business and the P.C. Rossin College of Engineering.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Boundaries between the three main undergraduate colleges are fluid, allowing students to cross-register or pursue integrated inter-college degrees like CSB and IDEAS.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants with varied, cross-disciplinary interests will find it much easier to explore multiple fields or change paths compared to more siloed peer institutions.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant 'work hard, play hard' mentality with strong school spirit, active Greek life, Division I athletics (Mountain Hawks), and over 400 student organizations.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: The campus is notoriously built on a mountain, consisting of three main campuses (Asa Packer, Mountaintop, and Murray H. Goodman). It requires significant walking, hiking, and use of campus transit.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: As an R1 institution, Lehigh provides extensive undergraduate research opportunities, with many professors welcoming first-year students into their labs and high-tech facilities.

current_status.test_policy_details: Lehigh has adopted an indefinite test-optional policy. Less than 40% of enrolled students in the Class of 2028 submitted test scores.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Lowest admit rate in five years; 10% rise in total applications.
Unusually high waitlist admit rate due to the COVID-19 pandemic and deferrals.

gpa.notes: 61% of enrolled freshmen placed in the top decile of their high school class; 86% were in the top quartile.

testing.policy_note: Data based on the middle 50% for the Class of 2029 profile. Only ~40% of the class submitted scores.

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students interested in natural sciences, social sciences, or humanities. The largest college at Lehigh, encompassing 37% of undergraduate enrollment.
colleges.student_archetype: Intellectually curious students seeking a classic liberal arts foundation paired with research.

colleges.name: P.C. Rossin College of Engineering and Applied Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Strong math/science students. It is significantly easier to transfer out of the engineering college than to transfer in, so undecided STEM students should start here.
colleges.student_archetype: Innovators and problem solvers; accounts for 25% of undergrads.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: The specialized CSB (Computer Science and Business) dual-degree requires transferring in with a minimum 3.25 cumulative GPA and a B- or better in ECO 001, CSE 007, and Calculus.

core_courses: CSE 140 Foundations of Discrete Structures and Algorithms
CSE 340 Design and Analysis of Algorithms

majors.notes: Computer Science is offered as a B.S. in Engineering, a B.A. or B.S. in Arts & Sciences, or as part of the highly selective CSB Honors Program.

colleges.name: College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students targeting corporate finance, accounting, or consulting. Accounts for 22% of undergraduates.

majors.name: Finance
majors.special_requirements: To internally transfer into the College of Business, a student must have 24 credits, a 2.0 GPA minimum, and a C- or better in ECO 001 and MATH 021/081.

electives: FIN 324 Security Analysis and Portfolio Management
FIN 334 Derivatives and Management of Risk

majors.notes: Finance majors must complete 2 foundation courses and 5 electives (at least 3 with FIN prefix).

colleges.name: Interdisciplinary Programs (IDEAS, CSB, IBE, IBH)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly ambitious students who have equally strong interests across multiple colleges (e.g., Engineering and Business). Admission is highly competitive.
colleges.student_archetype: High-achieving boundary-crossers; accounts for 11% of enrollment.

supplemental_requirements.details: Prompt 1: How did you first learn about Lehigh University and what motivated you to apply? (200 words). Prompt 2: Choice between reflecting on personal background and 'Inspiring the Future Makers' goals OR 'Personal Celebration' of something great in your life. (200-300 words).
Prompt 3: How will the unique combination of college, program, major and/or 4+1 program allow you to achieve your goals? (200 words).

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: Lehigh heavily tracks demonstrated interest. Evaluative interviews and campus visits are recorded and viewed as strong indicators of intent to enroll.

major_selection_tactics: Apply Early Decision: The ED acceptance rate (45%) is significantly higher than the RD rate, and Lehigh fills over 60% of its class during early rounds.
Select an Alternate Major: Lehigh specifically encourages applicants to list an alternate major, as you are applying directly to one of the undergraduate colleges.

Start in Engineering if Unsure: Because traditional engineering BS curricula lack flexibility, undecided STEM applicants are advised to start in the Rossin College of Engineering. It is easier to transfer out of engineering to Arts & Sciences than vice-versa.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying to highly selective interdisciplinary programs like CSB or IBE requires listing a backup college (e.g., Business or Engineering) on the application, which serves as an automatic fallback if rejected from the honors track.

scholarships.name: Soaring Together Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Full tuition for four years, cohort networking, leadership development.

scholarships.name: Founder's and Trustees' Scholarships
scholarships.benefits: Given to students in the top tier of the applicant pool; renewable with a 3.0 GPA.

scholarships.name: Dean's Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Recognizes academic excellence and demonstrated leadership.

scholarships.name: National Merit Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Awarded to National Merit Finalists who list Lehigh as their first choice.

scholarships.name: Performing Arts Scholarships in Theatre or Music
scholarships.benefits: Open to any major; requires active presence in the Department of Theatre/Music.

ap_policy.general_rule: Accepts scores of 4 or 5 for credit.
ap_policy.usage: Used for credit and placement, but credit is forfeited if the student takes the equivalent course at Lehigh.

ib_policy.general_rule: Grants credit for Higher Level (HL) subjects with scores of 5 or higher.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Courses must be from regionally accredited institutions, completed with a C or better (no pass/fail). Transfer credits do not calculate into the Lehigh GPA.

what_it_takes: Rigorous high school transcript featuring top-decile class rank.
Substantial Demonstrated Interest (campus tours, evaluative interviews).
Authentic and highly specific supplemental essays that tie deeply into Lehigh's interdisciplinary culture.
common_activities: Student-run interest groups (e.g., Cheese Club, Outing Club)
essay_tips: For the 'Why Lehigh' prompt, go beyond generic website research; mention specific dual-degree programs (CSB, IDEAS) or specific alumni/faculty interactions.
For the 'Personal Celebration' prompt, focus on a small, specific, and recent personal win rather than a massive academic accolade.
Connect your personal background to Lehigh's strategic 'Inspiring the Future Makers' goals (innovate, impact, collaborate).
red_flags: Zero demonstrated interest recorded by the admissions office.
Generic 'Why This College' essays that could apply to any mid-sized university.
Applying to engineering without strong grades in calculus, physics, and chemistry.
insights: The 'Lehigh Commitment' guarantees free tuition for families earning under $75,000, broadening the applicant pool.
Legacy applicants enjoy a notable advantage, with legacy ED admit rates hitting over 90% in recent cycles, though standards remain high.
